title: dianthus crinitus subsp. crinitus
intro: Dianthus crinitus subsp. crinitus, also known as the Fringed Pink, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The Fringed Pink has been associated with several health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
